deductions: Command dispatch incomplete, only detected 5/8 commands
Missing stack restore (Restore) logic for operation failures

IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.1 Logical correctness (15): 10/15 - Main loop dispatch mostly correct for single-char ops (X,C,+,*,%,@,D) and routes 'n' into a follow-up reader for "neg". PUSH/POP implement a downward-growing stack with empty at x3FF8 and full at x3FF3, and set R5=0/1 as required. Binary ops do POP/POP/compute/PUSH in the right operand order. However, stack restoration on error is incorrect: in OpAdd/OpMult/OpMod/OpXOR, the code attempts to "undo" pops with `ADD R6,R6,#-1` (which actually moves SP further down, not back up), and it never writes the popped values back, so the stack becomes corrupted on underflow or range errors. OpNeg does not range-check the negated result (negating -999 is fine, but general requirement says range check results). OpDisplay pops then reverts with `ADD R6,R6,#-1`, which is also the wrong direction and does not restore the value; display will corrupt the stack.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.2 Assembleability (10): 6/10 - Up to the truncation point, directives and instruction syntax look valid. Label usage for dispatch constants and subroutines is consistent. However, the submission is explicitly truncated at `ASCIItoBinary`/`BinarytoASCII` with `[...] truncated for grading ...]`, so as provided it will not assemble (missing code/labels, likely missing `.END`). Also, several comments claim LEA/LD are within ±255; without full layout it cannot be guaranteed, but the code shows attempts to keep data close.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.3 Edge case handling (10): 3/10 - RangeCheck logic for [-999,999] is essentially correct and reports an error, setting R5=1. Stack overflow/underflow are detected in PUSH/POP and print messages. But the required behavior "restore stack on error" is not met (incorrect SP adjustments and no value restoration). Modulo handles divisor=0 by printing an error, but again tries to restore by moving SP the wrong way. Input number parsing limits to 3 digits, but handling of negative values is inconsistent: PushValue stores '-' into ASCIIBUFF and then also separately negates the parsed result, which will only work if ASCIItoBinary ignores the '-' (unknown due to truncation). BinarytoASCII/ASCIItoBinary correctness cannot be verified due to truncation.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).4.4 Completeness (5): 3/5 - Implements exit (X), clear (C), add (+), multiply (*), modulo (%), XOR (@), negate (via "neg"), display (D), and number entry (PushValue). However, the conversion subroutines (ASCIItoBinary/BinarytoASCII) are not actually present in the submitted text (truncated), so the program is not complete/standalone as required.
IV. Code Quality LLM Judge (40 pts).LLM summary: Good overall structure (dispatch + stack abstractions) and correct basic POP/compute/PUSH flow, but major correctness issues: stack restoration is wrong throughout (SP moved the wrong way and values not restored), and display corrupts the stack. As submitted it also will not assemble due to truncate

# NOT(R3) AND 1 behavior:
# In LC-3 16-bit: NOT flips all bits. AND with immediate #1 = 0x0001.
# If R3=0: NOT=0xFFFF, AND 1 = 1
# If R3=1: NOT=0xFFFE, AND 1 = 0
# That is correct XOR with 1.

# Simulate OpMod
def mod_sim(a, b):
    if b == 0:
        return 'DIV_ZERO'
    sign = 1 if a >= 0 else -1
    aa = abs(a)
    bb = abs(b)
    # Repeated subtraction
    while aa >= bb:
        aa -= bb
    result = aa * sign
    return result

# Simulate XOR
def xor_sim(a, b):
    # ~a & b
    R3 = (~a) & b & 0xFFFF
    # a & ~b
    R4 = a & (~b) & 0xFFFF
    # NOT those two
    R3 = (~R3) & 0xFFFF
    R4 = (~R4) & 0xFFFF
    # AND
    R0 = R3 & R4
    # NOT
    R0 = (~R0) & 0xFFFF
    # Convert back to signed
    if R0 >= 0x8000:
        R0 -= 0x10000
    return R0

{"command": "python3 -c "
# LC-3 AND with immediate #1: imm5 = 00001 (sign-extended to 0x0001)
# This is valid (5-bit immediate range is -16 to 15, and 1 is in range)
# AND R3,R3,#1 -> R3 = R3 & 0x0001 (only keep bit 0)
# After NOT R3,R3: bit 0 flips. AND #1 keeps only bit 0.
# Result: bit 0 = ~(old bit 0)
# If R3 was 0 (0000...0): NOT -> 1111...1, AND #1 -> 0000...0001 = 1 ✓
# If R3 was 1 (0000...1): NOT -> 1111...0, AND #1 -> 0000...0000 = 0 ✓
print('LC-3 NOT+AND#1 toggle is valid')
